scope.goal: wire config resolve/build into backend startup so default agents are validated and registered at boot without introducing a global commander runtime

implemented: added explicit SQLite-backed config service initialization in cmd/codeflow-server startup flow
added registerConfiguredAgents startup helper that resolves main/coder/sub roles, reuses commander RoleFromConfigRole and BuildAgentFromResolved, and registers agent metadata into agent service
kept missing API channel backward compatible by skipping registration while still surfacing unsupported providers during startup
evidence: cmd/codeflow-server/main_test.go asserts three default roles are registered with idle status and model metadata
cmd/codeflow-server/main_test.go asserts missing API channel is skipped without startup failure
cmd/codeflow-server/main_test.go asserts unsupported provider causes registerConfiguredAgents to fail
cmd/codeflow-server/main.go now initializes config service and agent service before api.NewServer
risk: low startup wiring is additive, validates existing config semantics earlier, and avoids expanding Go ownership into TS runtime execution

notes: hook, skill, tool, commander, and cowork execution remain in TypeScript runtime/core
this slice only registers boot-time agent metadata and does not create a production global commander singleton
